focus: Final synthesis: research.md (17 sections) + resource-map.md + strategy convergence row + state append

title: Verification probe — fresh parser bash-only mode shows ~29 of 33 .sh files throw B1 before fresh parser itself crashes

data.context: single fresh parser, bash language only, no language interleaving

impact: Adds nuance to F-4.3 'B1 is content-conditional within bash, 9 of 33 throw': the 9-figure is iter-005 mixed-cohort steady-state (interleaving masks some throws); fresh-bash-only mode reveals broader bash-content-conditional set ~29 of 33. Skip-list seed should use the 70 production B1 paths (broadest safe), NOT the 9 (narrowest replay-stable subset).

note: No native binding installed. Reframed as web-tree-sitter × tree-sitter-wasms version-matrix question, only relevant for R-2 Phase-3 epic.
WASM module-level cumulative corruption from bash B1 stub-throws via missing external_scanner_reset export. Confidence 0.95.
Content matters only as bash-B1 fingerprint; no general syntactic pattern.
121 unique files; 70 B1 on .sh; 51 B2 across .sh/.ts/.py/.js.
run-all.sh family is reliable B1+B2 progenitor. Standalone <50-LOC fixture extraction deferred to post-research, not blocking Phase-2.

SQLite parser_skip_list table at v5; LRU cap 100; self-heal disabled by default; env flag SPECKIT_PARSER_SKIP_LIST_ENABLED default true; default seed = 70 production B1 paths backfilled from parse_diagnostics.
Parse-site try block tree-sitter-parser.ts:697-756; pre-check at :712; catch hook at :741-756. Schema migration at code-graph-db.ts:209-216. Diagnostics write surface at code-graph-db.ts:561-593.
